primary_complaint: Right shoulder pain and weakness following a work-related lifting injury
functional_limitations: Unable to lift objects above shoulder height
Cannot perform overhead reaching for daily tasks
Disturbed sleep due to shoulder pain
Unable to return to work duties requiring arm elevation
objective_measurements: Active forward flexion: 95 degrees (limited by pain)
Strength testing: 3/5 supraspinatus on empty can test

conservative_treatments_attempted.outcome: Minimal improvement, pain persisted with overhead activity
2 weeks of partial relief, then return to baseline symptoms
Inadequate pain control, GI intolerance developed

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (01/22/2025): AC joint arthrosis Grade II. No calcific deposits. No significant glenohumeral arthrosis.; MRI (01/22/2025): Full-thickness supraspinatus tear measuring 2.1 cm with retraction to the musculotendinous junction. Moderate subacromial bursitis. Intact subscapularis.

requested_procedure: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, right shoulder — CPT 29827
surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy End Date Uses Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The physical therapy course end date is recorded as 'October 2024', a placeholder token, which prevents verification of the exact treatment timeline. Payers may question whether the 8-week duration is accurate without a confirmed end date.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Confirm and document the exact end date of the physical therapy course to replace the 'October 2024' placeholder before submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: NSAID Course End Date Uses Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The NSAID (Meloxicam) course end date is recorded as 'October 2024', a placeholder token. Payers require confirmed treatment dates to validate the 3-month duration claim.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace 'October 2024' with the actual end date of the Meloxicam course to substantiate the 3-month duration for payer review.

denial_risk_flags.label: Corticosteroid Injection Date Is Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The corticosteroid injection date is recorded as '11/05/2024', a placeholder token. Payers require a specific administration date to confirm the injection occurred within an appropriate pre-surgical timeframe.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace '11/05/2024' with the actual injection date to confirm the timeline of conservative care before submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: Imaging Date Is Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Both MRI and X-ray dates are recorded as '01/22/2025', a placeholder token. Payers may require confirmed imaging dates to verify that studies were performed within an acceptable pre-authorization window.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace '01/22/2025' with the actual imaging date to ensure the studies are recognized as current and valid for authorization.

denial_risk_flags.label: Requesting Provider Name Is Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The attending surgeon is listed as 'Dr. Michael Torres', a placeholder token. Payers require a valid NPI-linked provider name on all prior authorization submissions.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace 'Dr. Michael Torres' with the attending surgeon's full legal name and NPI number before submitting the authorization request.

denial_risk_flags.label: Work Injury Origin May Require Workers' Comp Coordination
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart documents the injury as work-related, which may trigger a coordination-of-benefits review by Blue Cross Blue Shield to determine whether Workers' Compensation is the primary payer.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document whether a Workers' Compensation claim has been filed or denied, and confirm that BCBS is the appropriate primary payer for this procedure.

diagnosis_codes.note: Two ICD-10 codes are documented (M75.121 and M75.111), clearly supporting the diagnosis of full-thickness rotator cuff tear and shoulder impingement syndrome.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Three distinct conservative treatments are named: Physical Therapy, Corticosteroid Injection (Kenalog 40mg), and NSAIDs (Meloxicam 15mg daily), meeting the minimum threshold.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Step 1 — Build denominator (N): Duration-eligible treatments are Physical Therapy and NSAIDs (Meloxicam). The Corticosteroid Injection is a single-administration treatment and is excluded from N. N = 2. Step 2 — N = 2, which is ≥ 2, so proceed to Step 3. Step 3 — Build numerator (D): Physical Therapy has '8 weeks' — specific number plus time unit, counts. NSAIDs have '3 months' — specific number plus time unit, counts. D = 2. Step 4 — D/N = 2/2 = 100%, which is ≥ 50%, score = 1. 2 of 2 = 100%, meets threshold, score = 1.

imaging_findings.note: Both MRI and X-ray of the right shoulder are documented as completed with detailed findings, including a 2.1 cm full-thickness supraspinatus tear on MRI, directly supporting surgical necessity.

functional_limitations.note: Four specific functional limitations are explicitly documented, including inability to perform overhead work, disturbed sleep, and inability to return to work duties.

surgical_approach.note: Surgical approach is explicitly documented as 'Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair', providing sufficient detail for payer review.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 29827 (arthroscopic rotator cuff repair) is explicitly stated and is consistent with the documented diagnosis of full-thickness rotator cuff tear.

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration is explicitly stated as '8 months' in the medical necessity statement, providing a clear timeline of conservative management failure.
